Posted: Swift by Northwest Plans

These are a Few of My Stateful Machines

30 Jul 2017

“Trump is the essence of the GOP, distilled down to its depraved and odious core. … Have no doubt: There are more horrors to come.” —Paul Waldman

28 Jul 2017

“Every vote cast for Donald Trump was a vote for vulgarity. His supporters got exactly what they paid for.” —Bret Stephens

28 Jul 2017

Finally McCain’s actions match his rhetoric. “Senate Rejects Slimmed Down Obamacare Repeal as McCain Votes No”

27 Jul 2017

“But can we take a moment to consider the awfulness of Senator John McCain?” —Paul Krugman

27 Jul 2017

“It is a multitiered tower of political idiocy, a sublime monument to the moronic, a gaudy, gleaming, Ozymandian folly that leaves many of the president’s prior efforts in its shade.” —Ross Douthat

26 Jul 2017

In the end, McCain is reduced to merely an angry hypocrite. “[his] vote on Tuesday helped enable the broken process on health care that he came to the floor to decry” —WaPo

25 Jul 2017

Hey, Seattle. If you haven’t pick a mayoral candidate, please consider Jessyn Farrell, a brilliant transit/density wonk who can get things done. Just six days left to vote!

25 Jul 2017

It’s a beautiful night for a ballgame. Go Mariners.

24 Jul 2017

Under a monkey puzzle tree. /cc @tropicana_jones

23 Jul 2017

Our AG should be hanged for treason. “Sessions … has provided ‘misleading’ statements that are ‘contradicted by other evidence.’”

21 Jul 2017

“And if there is something bad, Mueller will find it. … In no scenario does Trump’s presidency recover.” —Jennifer Rubin

21 Jul 2017

Today’s work on OmniFocus [redacted] is brought to you by Archie Bell and the Drells. Tighten Up y’all.

18 Jul 2017

“Out of its collective sense of victimhood came the GOP’s disdain for not just intellectuals but also intellectualism, science, Economics 101, history and constitutional fidelity.” —Jennifer Rubin

15 Jul 2017

“I lose myself to the silences beyond noise and here I find myself rested in my working” —Paul Sellers

15 Jul 2017

“It is a party no longer capable of defending our national interests and Constitution from foreign enemies.”

14 Jul 2017

“Republicans’ willingness to accept even national betrayal … will disgrace the party and its leaders for years, if not permanently.” —Jennifer Rubin

14 Jul 2017

“Indeed, if the Russians didn’t approach the Trump circle…, it was probably because they recognized that anyone this naïve, giddy and … stupid would make a rather poor espionage partner.” —Ross Douthat

11 Jul 2017

Interesting. “What would transform politics would be combining ranked choice voting with multi-winner districts.” —Reihan Salam and Rob Ritchie

08 Jul 2017

“We believe Jessyn Farrell will do the best job advancing the best policies. She deserves your support for Mayor of Seattle.” —STB Editorial Board

07 Jul 2017
